condition.qualitative_description: Two or more severe drought weeks, or four or more moderate drought weeks in the latest kharif season, indicate meaningful crop-season water deficit even in a perhumid zone.

diagnostic_signals.explanation: In perhumid NE hill states, drought is episodic rather than chronic, but even short severe drought spells during critical crop growth stages (tillering, flowering) can cause significant yield loss. SPI-based drought week counts directly reflect meteorological deficit periods. Two or more severe weeks in a kharif season are sufficient to trigger agricultural drought stress in rain-fed hill farming systems where there is no supplemental irrigation buffer.

condition.qualitative_description: Three or more dry spell weeks (4-week moving window with <50% historical rainfall) in the latest kharif season indicate prolonged moisture stress periods that disrupt crop growth stages.

diagnostic_signals.explanation: Dry spells in perhumid hill zones are particularly damaging because farmers do not maintain irrigation infrastructure, relying entirely on in-season rainfall. A 4-week moving window with less than 50% of historical rainfall captures intra-seasonal dry spells that interrupt crop water supply at vulnerable growth stages. Three or more such weeks in a kharif season represent a significant departure from the normally reliable rainfall of AER-16 and are strongly associated with crop failure in rain-fed terraced fields.

condition.qualitative_description: SPI below -1.0 (moderate meteorological drought), MAI below 0.5 (soil moisture inadequacy), or VCI below 40 (moderate vegetation stress) in kharif season confirms drought causality per India Drought Manual classification.

diagnostic_signals.explanation: The India Drought Manual uses SPI, MAI, and VCI together to classify drought type and severity. SPI < -1.0 indicates meteorological drought; MAI < 0.5 indicates soil moisture inadequacy (agricultural drought); VCI < 40 indicates moderate-to-severe vegetation stress. In perhumid AER-16 zones, any single threshold breach is noteworthy given the normally high rainfall baseline of 2000-5000mm. The composite nature of this signal distinguishes true drought from localised crop management failures.

condition.qualitative_description: Kharif seasonal precipitation below 75% of the long-run annual mean, or a declining precipitation trend exceeding 20mm/year combined with mean annual rainfall below 1800mm, signals structural drought risk in this perhumid zone.

diagnostic_signals.explanation: AER-16 normally receives 2000-5000mm annually, so a kharif season receiving less than 75% of the long-run annual mean represents a substantial deficit. In hilly hard-rock terrain, rapid runoff means that even moderate rainfall deficits translate quickly into soil moisture stress because lateral drainage is fast and groundwater recharge in fractured hard-rock aquifers is spatially heterogeneous. A declining precipitation trend alongside below-normal mean annual rainfall suggests a structural shift rather than a single anomalous year.

condition.qualitative_description: Monsoon onset in the latest year is more than 7 days later than the earliest year in the record, indicating a systematic delay that shortens the effective kharif growing season.

diagnostic_signals.explanation: Delayed monsoon onset shortens the effective kharif season and increases the probability of terminal drought stress before crop maturity. In NE hill states, transplanting of rice and sowing of maize are tightly coupled to monsoon onset; a delay of more than one week forces either late planting (increasing exposure to post-monsoon dry spells) or abandonment of the season. This signal amplifies the drought pathway but does not confirm it independently.

confounders.confounder: Irrigation infrastructure failure or water diversion disputes causing apparent water scarcity without meteorological drought
confounders.how_to_distinguish: Check drought_causality_json for SPI and VCI values. If SPI is near-normal (> -0.5) but farmers report water scarcity, the cause is likely infrastructure failure or upstream diversion rather than meteorological drought. Confirm by checking whether dry_spell_weeks and drought_weeks_severe are also low; if all drought indices are near-normal, redirect diagnosis to irrigation infrastructure or water governance pathways.

confounders.confounder: Soil erosion and degraded water retention capacity on steep hilly terrain mimicking drought symptoms
confounders.how_to_distinguish: In hilly hard-rock terrain (terrain_cluster_id == 2 or similar slope indicators), degraded terraces and eroded soils can cause rapid runoff and poor soil moisture retention even in years with adequate rainfall. If seasonal_precipitation_mm is near-normal but drought_weeks_severe is elevated, check whether the signal is concentrated in early kharif (suggesting runoff-driven loss rather than rainfall deficit). Soil conservation works history (nrega_swc_count) can help distinguish: very low SWC investment combined with steep terrain suggests erosion-driven moisture loss rather than true meteorological drought.

sources.title: Management of crop water under drought: a review
sources.relevance_note: Reviews drought-induced crop water stress mechanisms and management options including phenology adaptation and water harvesting; supports the rationale for dry spell and drought week thresholds in hilly rainfed systems.

sources.title: Drought assessment for kharif rice using standardized precipitation index (SPI) and vegetation condition index (VCI)
sources.relevance_note: Demonstrates that SPI and VCI are positively correlated with kharif rice productivity index; validates use of SPI < -1.0 and VCI < 40 thresholds for diagnosing agricultural drought in kharif season.

sources.title: Drought Management Manual
authors: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
sources.relevance_note: Defines India's official drought classification using SPI, MAI, and VCI; establishes thresholds for moderate and severe drought categories used in sig_03 composite condition.

sources.title: Water-food-energy nexus with changing agricultural scenarios in India during recent decades
sources.relevance_note: Documents how post-drought groundwater storage fails to recover even after good monsoon years in India, and highlights the importance of seasonal precipitation patterns for diagnosing agricultural water stress.
